S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — THE CORE CONCEPT: WHAT THE GAT

data.title: THE CORE CONCEPT: WHAT THE GATE ACTUALLY IS
blocks: · Proofreading. Checking grammar and flow tells you whether the output is readable, not whether it is true.
· Admiring the format. A clean table can contain wrong denominators. A beautiful chart can mislead through axis choice. A
· Asking Claude to check itself. Asking "is this correct?" can help surface some issues, but it is not independent verific

Why Fluency Is Not a Verification Signal — Farquhar et al. (Nature, 2024) demonstrated this technically by measuring semantic entropy — the spread of meaning acros
data.section: WHY FLUENCY IS NOT A VERIFICAT

data.title: THE VERIFICATION MATRIX.
blocks: Summary — Unsupported or reversed claims
Citation — Fabricated or irrelevant source
Number or table — Wrong denominator, formula error, rounding
Chart or figure — Misleading encoding, overclaiming caption

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — WORKED EXAMPLE: AUDITING A LIT

data.title: WORKED EXAMPLE: AUDITING A LITERATURE SUMMARY
blocks: What Claude returns:
Step 1: Split into claims.
Step 2: Assign evidence type.
Step 3: Check a sample.
Step 4: Mark each claim.

data.title: EDITING VERSUS AUDITING.
blocks: This distinction matters enough to state plainly.
Editing Versus Auditing — Editing asks: Is the output clear, appropriately framed, well-organized, and suitable for the audience?

data.title: RISK-TIERED GATING
blocks: · Skim the output for obvious errors.
· Does the overall structure match your intent?
· Are there any claims that would embarrass you if wrong?
· Take ten seconds, then proceed.
· Apply the verification matrix for each output type present.

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: "I read it and it sounded right."
"Claude cited it, so it's real."
"The table looks clean."
"The chart is beautiful."
"A passing test means the code is complete."

data.title: THE ONE RULE.
blocks: The research notes for this chapter propose one rule that survives every edge case:
The One Rule — No verification path, no delegation.
